title: Nartian

{{Quote|Wuher. We've got a positive on the droid detector.|Nackhar|<ref name="Wuher">{{StoryCite|story=Be Still My Heart: The Bartender's Tale|book=Tales from the Mos Eisley Cantina}}</ref>}}
The '''Nartians''' were a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] of short, four-[[Limb/Legends|armed]] beings. Native to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Nars]] in the [[Senex sector/Legends|Senex sector]], they were frequently employed as household servants for the nobles of the region, sometimes serving the same noble family for generations. The Nartian [[Nackhar]] worked in [[Chalmun's Spaceport Cantina/Legends|Chalmun's Spaceport Cantina]] on [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]] and was present when [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] and [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] visited the [[Cantina/Legends|cantina]] seeking passage off the planet.

==Biology and appearance==
The Nartians were a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]], whose members had four [[Limb/Legends|arms]] each. They were slightly smaller than [[Human/Legends|Humans]] in stature<ref name="SWE" /> and scurried when they walked.<ref name="Wuher" />

==Society and culture==
The Nartians had a number of celebrations and rites of ancient origins, which were celebrated during [[Nars Week]]. These were of such significance that many [[Noble/Legends|nobles]] of the [[Senex sector/Legends|Senex]]-[[Juvex sector/Legends|Juvex sector]] region who had Nartian [[Slavery/Legends|servants]] gave them permission to partake in the ceremonies. All Nartians were expected to make a pilgrimage to their ancestral [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] [[Nars]] at least once in their lifetime. Elderly Nartians frequently spent significant portions of their savings for that purpose.<ref name="Atlas" />

==History==
The Nartians evolved on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] Nars, a pale, chilly world located in the Senex sector of the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]]. For [[Time/Legends|millennia]], they served as household servants for the nobles of the region, becoming ubiquitous across both the Senex and Juvex sectors. Often Nartian families served the same noble family for generations.<ref name="Atlas" /> Some Nartians also made it into the wider [[The galaxy/Legends|galaxy]], beyond the borders of the Senex-Juvex sectors, finding work independent of the noble families of their region.<ref name="Wuher" />

==Nartians in the galaxy==
During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], the Nartian [[Nackhar]] lived on the planet [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]],<ref name="Wuher" /> located in the [[Arkanis sector/Legends|Arkanis sector]].<ref name="Atlas" /> In [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]],<ref name="TERC">''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' dates the events of "[[Nightlily: The Lovers' Tale]]" and of "[[Be Still My Heart: The Bartender's Tale]]" to [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]].</ref> he worked two jobs: as a [[fast food]] vendor in the [[Mos Eisley/Legends|Mos Eisley]] marketplace<ref name="Nightlily">{{StoryCite|story=Nightlily: The Lovers' Tale|book=Tales from the Mos Eisley Cantina}}</ref> and as an assistant [[Bartender/Legends|bartender]] to [[Wuher/Legends|Wuher]] in [[Chalmun's Spaceport Cantina/Legends|Chalmun's Spaceport Cantina]]. He was present on the day when local residents [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] and [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] entered the [[Cantina/Legends|cantina]]<ref name="Wuher" /> and met the [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]], setting in motion a chain of events that would lead to Skywalker destroying the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]].<ref name="Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope">{{Film|IV}}</ref>

[[File:Pondababa.png|thumb|right|250px|Nackhar cleaned the blood and severed limb of Ponda Baba (pictured).]]
On that [[Standard day/Legends|day]], Skywalker and Kenobi were accompanied by two [[Droid/Legends|droids]], who were forbidden to enter the cantina. Noticing the [[Droid detector/Legends|droid detector]] going off as the droids entered, Nackhar alerted Wuher to their presence and the bartender then ordered them to leave. Shortly after that, another patron, [[Ponda Baba/Legends|Ponda Baba]], drew his [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] to attack Skywalker after little provocation. Before he could complete his assault, Kenobi ignited his [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] and severed Baba's blaster arm. Following the altercation, Nackhar was assigned to clean up the [[Blood/Legends|blood]] and severed limb.<ref name="Wuher" />

Later that day, Solo killed the [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]] [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Greedo/Legends|Greedo]]. At Wuher's behest, the Nartian claimed the corpse for [[Chalmun/Legends|Chalmun's]] Cantina, fighting off scavenging [[Jawa/Legends|Jawas]] who also desired the body. Wuher later used the corpse as an ingredient in [[Alcohol/Legends|liqueur]] for [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba]] the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]].<ref name="Wuher" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Nartians first appeared with the character Nackhar in the short stories "[[Be Still My Heart: The Bartender's Tale]]" and "[[Nightlily: The Lovers' Tale]]," published in the [[1995]] anthology ''[[Tales from the Mos Eisley Cantina]]''.<ref name="Wuher" /><ref name="Nightlily" /> The stories were respectively authored by [[David Bischoff]]<ref name="Wuher" /> and [[Barbara Hambly]],<ref name="Nightlily" /> with Bischoff's work being the first to introduce the species' name.<ref name="Wuher" />
